suggestions.title: Stop v0_94 from self-targeting and farming monsters instead of lane minions
suggestions.description: The fresh `train-diy-v0_94` metrics show `attack_action_target_self_rate` around 13%, monster targeting around 9%, enemy-hero targeting near 0.5%, and `rwd_last_hit` deeply negative. Codex can trace the target mask/build path and patch the agent so lane attacks stop collapsing onto self and neutral targets.
suggestions.prompt: Inspect the new `train-diy-v0_94` run in this repo and fix why the DIY agent is still self-targeting and hitting monsters instead of lane minions or the enemy hero. Start from the new target-joint metrics and patch the relevant target mask / feature / action-selection code, then explain the concrete root cause and the code change.

suggestions.title: Rebalance the training opponent mix for common-ai lane pressure before selfplay takes over
suggestions.description: Your current branch already added dynamic opponent curriculum, but the live `train-diy-v0_94` run is still weak on the lane objectives you care about. Codex can tune `train_opponent_mix` and its switching thresholds so the agent spends more time learning the standard common-AI laning flow before selfplay dominates.
suggestions.prompt: Use the current repo state and the latest `train-diy-v0_94` metrics to retune the DIY training opponent curriculum. I want the agent to first learn stable common-AI laning and last-hit pressure, then transition to more selfplay. Patch the config/workflow, justify the new thresholds, and point out exactly what was wrong with the current mix.

suggestions.title: Turn the new v0_94 pull script output into a regression diff against v0_92
suggestions.description: You just added `pull_training_data.py`, and there is now fresh data for `train-diy-v0_94` plus older `train-diy-v0_92`. Codex can produce a focused diff on the metrics that changed because of today’s AdaLN, entropy, monitor, and curriculum edits, so you know which edit helped and which one hurt.
suggestions.prompt: Compare the local training artifacts for `train-diy-v0_94` and `train-diy-v0_92` and give me a regression-focused report tied to today’s code changes. I care about whether the AdaLN/entropy/curriculum edits improved learning or just changed the failure mode. Use the existing pull script and local summaries, and highlight the few code paths I should change next.

suggestions.title: Fix ppo-0-5 never sending samples to the learner
suggestions.description: Today's `ppo-0-5` run finished with `train_global_step=0`, `sample_receive_cnt=0`, and 1,299 fresh `policy.send_train_data failed` warnings in `logs/ppo-0-5`. Codex can trace the swapped PPO sampling path and patch the breakage before your next run.
suggestions.prompt: 请你直接排查并修复 `ppo-0-5` 这次训练没有把样本送到 learner 的问题。重点看 `logs/ppo-0-5/summary.json`、`logs/ppo-0-5/errors.jsonl` 里反复出现的 `policy.send_train_data failed`，以及 `agent_ppo/feature/definition.py`、`agent_ppo/agent.py`、`agent_ppo/workflow/train_workflow.py`、`agent_ppo/algorithm/algorithm.py` 这条采样链路。我要的是能继续正常训练的代码改动，不是只做原因分析。

suggestions.title: Stop train-diy-v0_97 spending half its attacks on self targets
suggestions.description: Your newest DIY run still shows `attack_action_target_self_rate=0.5276` and `attack_action_target_none_rate=0.1747` in `logs/train-diy-v0_97/summary.json`, so the policy is still burning actions after the earlier target-ordering fix. Codex can trace the remaining self-target path and patch the mask or target semantics before `v0_98`.
suggestions.prompt: 请你根据 `logs/train-diy-v0_97/summary.json` 直接定位并修复为什么最新的 diy 训练里 `attack_action_target_self_rate` 还是高达 0.5276、`attack_action_target_none_rate` 也很高。重点检查 `agent_diy/feature/action_mask.py`、`agent_diy/feature/definition.py`、`agent_diy/feature/targeting.py` 和相关测试，给我一个可落地的代码修复，目标是减少自目标和空目标攻击。

suggestions.title: Port DIY diagnostics into the PPO feature-swap run
suggestions.description: The current PPO swap only exposes the 37 platform metrics, so today's `ppo-0-5` run hides `is_train_rate`, head entropies, and reward-item breakdowns that your DIY runs already rely on. Codex can mirror those diagnostics in `agent_ppo` so the next architecture-isolation run is actually interpretable.
suggestions.prompt: 请你把 `agent_diy` 里已经有的训练诊断能力补到现在这个 `agent_ppo` 的 feature-swap 版本上，至少包括 `is_train_rate`、各 head entropy、reward 分项和关键动作/target 统计。参考 `agent_diy/conf/monitor_builder.py`、`agent_diy/workflow/train_workflow.py`、`agent_diy/algorithm/algorithm.py`，直接改 `agent_ppo` 对应文件，并补最必要的测试，让我下一次跑 PPO 架构替换实验时能看清到底是哪里没学到。

suggestions.title: Make ppo-1-2 actually trigger low-HP recall
suggestions.description: Today's `ppo-1-2` run still shows `recall_need_cnt≈188` but `recall_start_cnt=0`, `recall_success_cnt=0`, and `action_button_9=0` after commits `19fd508` and `3463df5`. Trace the train and eval paths, patch why Button9 never fires in real rollouts, and lock it down with tests around the behind-own-tower case.
suggestions.prompt: 检查当前项目里 `ppo-1-2` 的回城链路。最新日志里 `recall_need_cnt` 很高，但 `recall_start_cnt`、`recall_success_cnt`、`action_button_9` 仍然是 0，说明我今天加的回城 reward / exploration / legal mask 还没有真正让训练采到回城。请你沿着 `agent_ppo/agent.py`、`agent_ppo/feature/action_mask.py`、`agent_ppo/feature/reward_process.py` 和现有测试把问题定位清楚，直接修代码并补测试，目标是让低血脱战、尤其己方塔后安全位置时，训练 rollout 里真的能出现回城启动与保持。

suggestions.title: Expose the missing recall exploration metrics locally
suggestions.description: `monitor_builder.py` now defines `recall_explore_*` metrics, but the fresh `logs/ppo-1-2/summary.json` only surfaces `recall_button_rate_when_needed`, so the current experiment hides whether the override path ever ran. Fix the local pull/summary pipeline so recall ablations are readable without reopening the Kaiwu dashboard.
suggestions.prompt: 帮我检查为什么当前项目里已经在 `agent_ppo/conf/monitor_builder.py` 定义了 `recall_explore_*` 指标，但 `logs/ppo-1-2/summary.json` 里几乎看不到这些字段，导致我现在无法判断临时回城探索到底有没有触发。请你把本地拉取与汇总链路查清楚，必要时修改 `script/pull_training_data.py` 或相关逻辑，让这些指标稳定落到 summary 里，并顺手回填/验证当前 `ppo-1-2` 的输出。

suggestions.title: Defuse the minion penalty dominating PPO lane behavior
suggestions.description: Across the latest PPO runs, `rwd_minion_hp_point` is still around `-10` to `-12`, much larger than tower progress, and `ppo-1-2` also dropped on last-hit and frontline presence. Inspect whether this term is overpowering the intended 1v1 lane policy and prepare a minimal reward patch for the next run.
suggestions.prompt: 请你针对当前项目的 PPO reward 再做一次面向修复的检查，不是只分析。重点看 `rwd_minion_hp_point` 在最近几次 PPO 日志里都在 `-10` 到 `-12` 左右，明显压过了推塔和很多正向收益，而且 `ppo-1-2` 的补刀与前场存在也掉了。请你沿着 `agent_ppo/feature/reward_process.py` 把这个惩罚项的真实触发机制和量级查清楚，判断它是否在把策略往错误方向推，然后直接给出最小可验证的 reward patch 和对应测试。

suggestions.title: Finish the no-recall PPO patch before your next remote run
suggestions.description: Your working tree is still dirty in `agent_ppo`, and the last several local commits all revolve around recall shaping. Codex can reconcile the disabled recall path, keep the useful low-HP retreat logic, and leave you with a tested patch that is ready to sync.
suggestions.prompt: Work in `/Users/wjx/code/kaiwu`. Finish the current `agent_ppo` no-recall patch coherently. Read `AGENTS.md`, inspect the dirty `agent_ppo` files and recent recall-related commits, then make the code consistent with `RECALL_ENABLED = False`: remove or gate any remaining recall-only reward/mask/assist behavior that should not affect training, keep the useful low-HP retreat logic, update tests and monitor expectations, run the relevant targeted tests, and summarize exactly what is safe to sync to Kaiwu WebIDE.

suggestions.title: Run a short Kaiwu WebIDE A/B for the probe-backed `agent_diy`
suggestions.description: This repo already has `diag_feature_probes`, `agent_diy` regression tests, and WebIDE sync/pull scripts, while your recent threads show local training is blocked. Codex can push a minimal payload, run a short remote smoke train or eval, and pull back the metrics needed to tell whether the new features are actually helping.

Use the existing remote tooling (`script/kaiwu_remote.py`, `script/pull_training_data.py`, and any related docs/scripts in the repo) to prepare and execute a minimal Kaiwu WebIDE smoke test for `agent_diy`, based on the current probe-backed feature implementation. Sync only what is needed, run a short eval or training sanity check remotely, pull the key metrics/logs back, and give me a compact verdict on whether the new `agent_diy` features look healthy enough to justify a longer run.

suggestions.title: Port the proven `agent_diy` probe fixes into the PPO agent
suggestions.description: You are actively tuning `agent_ppo`, but the probe-backed ability, bullet, and attack-target logic already exists and is tested in `agent_diy`. Codex can extract the smallest high-value parity patch for PPO and add matching tests before you spend more remote training time on a weaker observation stack.

Compare `agent_diy` and `agent_ppo` from the perspective of the probe-backed observation improvements already implemented in `agent_diy` (especially hero abilities, semantic `attack_target`, bullet handling, and any other confirmed high-signal features). Identify the smallest subset that should be ported into `agent_ppo` now, implement that parity patch with focused tests, run verification, and explain which improvements were intentionally deferred.

suggestions.title: Finish the final submission bundle before the June 30 deadline

suggestions.description: `main.tex` still says the working tree had uncommitted PPO changes, but the repo is clean now and HEAD is the June 24 snapshot. Codex can patch the paper narrative and refresh any analysis text that still depends on the old dirty-tree assumption.

suggestions.title: Prepare a defense-ready tokenization note for the Uzi poster
suggestions.description: Today's Codex thread asked why this model needs tokenization, and your paper/poster already hinge on structured entity tokens plus the hybrid token-LSTM policy. Codex can turn the actual feature builder and model code into a short Chinese/English explanation you can reuse during poster Q&A.
